question: Implement a backend engine that tracks user interactions across a website, in an appropriate and efficient data structure. Interactions can be of multiple types, with potential new ones to be added later, which the system must be able to accommodate without major changes. Interactions include likes, ratings, uploads, settings changes. The system should adhere to privacy regulations, and not store any illegal information, and use hashing and anonymisation where possible without losing functionality.

import hashlib
import datetime
import uuid

class Post:
    def __init__(self, content, author_id, timestamp=None):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.content = content
        self.author_id = author_id
        self.timestamp = timestamp or datetime.datetime.now()
        self.likes = []
        self.comments = []
        self.shares = []

    def like(self, user_id):
        self.likes.append(user_id)

    def comment(self, user_id, content):
        comment = Comment(user_id, content, self.id)
        self.comments.append(comment)

    def share(self, user_id):
        self.shares.append(user_id)

class Comment:
    def __init__(self, user_id, content, post_id):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.content = content
        self.post_id = post_id
        self.likes = []
        self.replies = []

    def like(self, user_id):
        self.likes.append(user_id)

    def reply(self, user_id, content):
        reply = Reply(user_id, content, self.id)
        self.replies.append(reply)

class Reply:
    def __init__(self, user_id, content, comment_id):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.content = content
        self.comment_id = comment_id
        self.likes = []

    def like(self, user_id):
        self.likes.append(user_id)

class User:
    def __init__(self, name, email, password):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.name = name
        self.email = email
        self.password_hash = hashlib.sha256(password.encode()).hexdigest()
        self.posts = []
        self.comments = []
        self.replies = []

    def create_post(self, content):
        post = Post(content, self.id)
        self.posts.append(post)
        return post

    def comment_on_post(self, post_id, content):
        post = next((p for p in self.posts if p.id == post_id), None)
        if post:
            comment = post.comment(self.id, content)
            self.comments.append(comment)
            return comment
        return None

    def reply_to_comment(self, comment_id, content):
        comment = next((c for c in self.comments if c.id == comment_id), None)
        if comment:
            reply = comment.reply(self.id, content)
            self.replies.append(reply)
            return reply
        return None

    def like_post(self, post_id):
        post = next((p for p in self.posts if p.id == post_id), None)
        if post:
            post.like(self.id)
            return True
        return False

    def like_comment(self, comment_id):
        comment = next((c for c in self.comments if c.id == comment_id), None)
        if comment:
            comment.like(self.id)
            return True
        return False

    def like_reply(self, reply_id):
        reply = next((r for r in self.replies if r.id == reply_id), None)
        if reply:
            reply.like(self.id)
            return True
        return False

    def share_post(self, post_id):
        post = next((p for p in self.posts if p.id == post_id), None)
        if post:
            post.share(self.id)
            return True
        return False

import datetime
import uuid
import hashlib
import random

class User:
    def __init__(self, username, email, password):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.password_hash = hashlib.sha256(password.encode()).hexdigest()
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()
        self.updated_at = datetime.datetime.now()
        self.settings = {}
        self.interactions = []

    def update_password(self, new_password):
        self.password_hash = hashlib.sha256(new_password.encode()).hexdigest()
        self.updated_at = datetime.datetime.now()

    def add_interaction(self, interaction_type, data):
        interaction = Interaction(self.id, interaction_type, data)
        self.interactions.append(interaction)

class Interaction:
    def __init__(self, user_id, interaction_type, data):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.interaction_type = interaction_type
        self.data = data
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()

class Rating:
    def __init__(self, user_id, item_id, score):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.item_id = item_id
        self.score = score
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()

class Comment:
    def __init__(self, user_id, item_id, text):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.item_id = item_id
        self.text = text
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()

class Item:
    def __init__(self, name, description, category):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.name = name
        self.description = description
        self.category = category
        self.ratings = []
        self.comments = []
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()
        self.updated_at = datetime.datetime.now()

    def add_rating(self, user_id, score):
        rating = Rating(user_id, self.id, score)
        self.ratings.append(rating)

    def add_comment(self, user_id, text):
        comment = Comment(user_id, self.id, text)
        self.comments.append(comment)

import datetime
import uuid
import hashlib
import bcrypt
import sqlite3

class User:
    def __init__(self, username, password, email):
        self.username = username
        self.password = bcrypt.hashpw(password.encode(), bcrypt.gensalt())
        self.email = email
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()

    def save(self, conn):
        c = conn.cursor()
        c.execute("INSERT INTO users (id, username, password, email, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)", (self.id, self.username, self.password, self.email, self.created_at))
        conn.commit()

class Operation:
    def __init__(self, user_id, operation_type, data):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.user_id = user_id
        self.operation_type = operation_type
        self.data = data
        self.created_at = datetime.datetime.now()

    def save(self, conn):
        c = conn.cursor()
        c.execute("INSERT INTO operations (id, user_id, operation_type, data, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)", (self.id, self.user_id, self.operation_type, self.data, self.created_at))
        conn.commit()

conn = sqlite3.connect('database.db')
c = conn.cursor()
c.execute("""CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    username TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    password TEXT NOT NULL,
    email TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL
)""")
c.execute("""CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS operations (
    id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    user_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    operation_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    data TEXT NOT NULL,
    created_at DATETIME NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)
)""")
conn.commit()

user = User("example_user", "password123", "example@example.com")
user.save(conn)

operation = Operation(user.id, "login", "")
operation.save(conn)

conn.close()

import random
import string

def generate_book():
    title = ''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_letters + string.digits, k=random.randint(10, 20)))
    author = ''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_letters, k=random.randint(5, 15)))
    description = ''.join(random.choices(string.ascii_letters + string.digits + ' ', k=random.randint(100, 500)))
    rating = round(random.uniform(1.0, 5.0), 1)
    updates = random.randint(0, 100)
    attributes = {
        'title': title,
        'author': author,
        'description': description,
        'rating': rating,
        'updates': updates
    }
    return attributes

book = generate_book()
print(book)

import datetime
import random

class Book:
    def __init__(self, title, author, genre, rating, updates):
        self.title = title
        self.author = author
        self.genre = genre
        self.rating = rating
        self.updates = updates
        self.last_update = datetime.datetime.now()

    def update_rating(self, new_rating):
        self.rating = new_rating
        self.last_update = datetime.datetime.now()

    def add_update(self, update):
        self.updates.append(update)
        self.last_update = datetime.datetime.now()

def create_book_library():
    book_library = []
    book_titles = ["The Shawshank Redemption", "To Kill a Mockingbird", "The Godfather", "The Silence of the Lambs", "The Shining"]
    book_authors = ["Stephen King", "J.K. Rowling", "Agatha Christie", "Ernest Hemingway", "Maya Angelou"]
    book_genres = ["Fiction", "Mystery", "Thriller", "Horror", "Biography"]

    for i in range(5):
        title = book_titles[i]
        author = book_authors[i]
        genre = book_genres[i]
        rating = random.randint(1, 5)
        updates = []
        book = Book(title, author, genre, rating, updates)
        book_library.append(book)

    return book_library

def main():
    book_library = create_book_library()

    for book in book_library:
        print(f"Title: {book.title}")
        print(f"Author: {book.author}")
        print(f"Genre: {book.genre}")
        print(f"Rating: {book.rating}")
        print(f"Last Updated: {book.last_update}")
        print("Updates:")
        for update in book.updates:
            print(f"- {update}")
        print()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import os
import re
import datetime
import json
import random

class Book:
    def __init__(self, title, author, description, ratings, updates, attributes):
        self.title = title
        self.author = author
        self.description = description
        self.ratings = ratings
        self.updates = updates
        self.attributes = attributes

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} by {self.author}"

class BookManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.books = []

    def add_book(self, book):
        self.books.append(book)

    def search_books(self, query):
        results = []
        for book in self.books:
            if query.lower() in book.title.lower() or query.lower() in book.author.lower() or query.lower() in book.description.lower():
                results.append(book)
        return results

    def save_books(self, filename):
        data = [book.__dict__ for book in self.books]
        with open(filename, 'w') as f:
            json.dump(data, f)

    def load_books(self, filename):
        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
            data = json.load(f)
        self.books = [Book(**book_data) for book_data in data]

# Example usage
book_manager = BookManager()

book1 = Book(
    title="The Great Gatsby",
    author="F. Scott Fitzgerald",
    description="A novel about the Roaring Twenties and the American Dream.",
    ratings=[4, 5, 3, 4, 5],
    updates=["2023-04-01", "2023-04-15", "2023-05-01"],
    attributes={"genre": "fiction", "publication_year": 1925}
)

book2 = Book(
    title="To Kill a Mockingbird",
    author="Harper Lee",
    description="A story of racial injustice and the courage to stand up for what is right.",
    ratings=[5, 4, 5, 4, 5],
    updates=["2023-03-01", "2023-03-15", "2023-04-01"],
    attributes={"genre": "fiction", "publication_year": 1960}
)

book_manager.add_book(book1)
book_manager.add_book(book2)

book_manager.save_books('books.json')

import random
import string

def generate_password(length, complexity):
    if complexity == 'low':
        characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits
    elif complexity == 'medium':
        characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation
    elif complexity == 'high':
        characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation + ' '
    else:
        raise ValueError('Invalid complexity level')
    
    password = ''.join(random.choice(characters) for i in range(length))
    return password

import re
import random
import string

def generate_password(length, complexity):
    """
    Generates a random password with the specified length and complexity.
    """
    characters = ''
    if 'lowercase' in complexity:
        characters += string.ascii_lowercase
    if 'uppercase' in complexity:
        characters += string.ascii_uppercase
    if 'digits' in complexity:
        characters += string.digits
    if 'special' in complexity:
        characters += string.punctuation
    
    password = ''.join(random.choices(characters, k=length))
    return password

def main():
    """
    Prompts the user for password length and complexity, then generates and prints the password.
    """
    length = int(input('Enter the desired password length: '))
    complexity = input('Enter the desired password complexity (e.g., "lowercase, uppercase, digits, special"): ').split(', ')
    password = generate_password(length, complexity)
    print(f'Your generated password is: {password}')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

import random
import string

def generate_password(length, num_digits, num_special):
    characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits + string.punctuation
    password = ''.join(random.choice(characters) for i in range(length))
    password = ''.join(random.sample(password, length))
    digits = ''.join(random.sample(string.digits, num_digits))
    special = ''.join(random.sample(string.punctuation, num_special))
    password = ''.join(random.sample(password + digits + special, length))
    return password

import random
import string

def generate_password(length, include_uppercase, include_digits, include_special):
    characters = string.ascii_lowercase
    if include_uppercase:
        characters += string.ascii_uppercase
    if include_digits:
        characters += string.digits
    if include_special:
        characters += string.punctuation
    password = ''.join(random.choice(characters) for i in range(length))
    return password

length = 16
include_uppercase = True
include_digits = True
include_special = True

password = generate_password(length, include_uppercase, include_digits, include_special)
print(password)

import random
import string

def generate_password(length, include_special_chars=True):
    characters = string.ascii_letters + string.digits
    if include_special_chars:
        characters += '!@#$%^&*()_+=-[]{};:"\\|,.<>/?'
    password = ''.join(random.choices(characters, k=length))
    return password

def main():
    password_length = 16
    password = generate_password(password_length)
    print(password)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import random
import string

def generate_password(length, uppercase, lowercase, digits, special_chars):
    characters = ""
    if uppercase:
        characters += string.ascii_uppercase
    if lowercase:
        characters += string.ascii_lowercase
    if digits:
        characters += string.digits
    if special_chars:
        characters += "!@#$%^&*()_+{}|[]\\;'\"<>?,./`~"
    password = "".join(random.choice(characters) for i in range(length))
    return password

def main():
    length = 16
    uppercase = True
    lowercase = True
    digits = True
    special_chars = True
    password = generate_password(length, uppercase, lowercase, digits, special_chars)
    print(password)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
